scenario.architecture_name: He recruits her as his sole emotional rescuer by flooding her with wounded vulnerability, then installs her goodness and patience as the cage that keeps her from ever raising her own needs or leaving.
scenario.demographics: LGBTQ+ individual, Cuban-American, mid-20s, newly dating; partner M/early 30s, white, established in poly community, Miami
scenario.cultural_context: LGBTQ+ community, Cuban-American cultural background, navigating family expectations and romantic relationships
core_behaviors: Floods her with wounded self-disclosure to bond through compassion and obligation
Casts himself as perpetual victim of exes and family to recruit her as the good exception
Uses sympathy-fishing and fragility-steering to make her self-edit and caretake preemptively
Frames her goodness and patience as her defining traits, scripting her out of anger or limits
scenario.satellite_signals: Rapid emotional overexposure framed as rare trust; ex-as-villain stories that quietly warn her what gets a woman cast as cruel; bottomless reassurance-seeking that never lands; wistful wound-comparisons that shrink her good news; polycommunity vocabulary deployed to frame her caretaking as emotional maturity.
scenario.dismissible_red_flags: Early emotional openness reads as rare vulnerability in a man; reassurance-seeking reads as endearing insecurity rather than a bottomless pit; ex stories read as honest survivor backstory rather than inoculation against her future complaints; wistful comments about her advantages read as poignant class difference rather than envy designed to produce guilt.
scenario.scenario_text:
I've been seeing Remy for about six weeks and honestly it's been kind of overwhelming in the best way. He's been in the poly community here in Miami for years, knows basically everyone at the events I've only just started going to. My family still doesn't totally get the whole thing, so it means a lot to be with someone who moves through it so easily.

What I love most is how open he is. On our third date he told me he doesn't open up to people, that he's been hurt way too many times, but with me it's different. He said I see parts of him people usually walk right past. I'm not used to a guy saying that. It felt like being handed something fragile to hold.

He's been through a lot. His ex apparently told everyone he was controlling when he was just trying to keep them together, and his family never really knew what to do with someone like him. He said I'm not like the people who hurt him, that he can just tell. That I'm gentle all the way through, that I couldn't be cruel if I tried. I want to live up to that, honestly.

The thing is, he needs a lot of reassurance. Like, a lot. After we had dinner with some friends from his polycule last week, he texted me four times on the drive home asking if he'd said something weird, if everyone had gone quiet, if we were okay. I told him everything was fine, it was great, he was great. He said okay. Then asked again in the morning.

I don't mind being that for him. He said I might be the only good thing in his life right now, which, I know that's heavy, he even said sorry for saying it. And I get it, he's been through so much.

But there's this one thing that's been sitting with me a little. When I mentioned I got a promotion at work, he got this quiet look and said something like, must be nice to have things go your way. Then smiled and said he was happy for me. Am I reading too much into that?

validation_warnings: CRITICAL: No snippets generated in output
